pattern: A prefix-sum array turns every fixed-width window sum into a single subtraction, so instead of re-summing each radius-k window from scratch you slide a constant-time window across the array.
hints: Recomputing the sum of each window from scratch is wasteful -- notice that consecutive windows overlap almost entirely.
Build a running prefix-sum array first; then the sum of any window [i-k, i+k] is just prefix[i+k+1] - prefix[i-k], computed in O(1).

Only indices with at least k elements on both sides can have a real average -- everything else is -1 -- and remember the division truncates toward zero, which for non-negative sums is just ordinary integer division.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, middle indices have valid averages
statement example, k = 0 means every index is its own window
statement example, k larger than the array, everything is -1
k exactly half of a small array, only the center index qualifies
k one more than fits, nothing qualifies
window sum not evenly divisible, checks truncation toward zero
all zeros, every valid average is exactly zero
large uniform values near the constraint ceiling to check overflow-safe summing
k=0 on a multi-element array returns nums unchanged

stress.description: random arrays with a random radius, sometimes larger than the array so most or all entries are -1
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [nums, k]
